episode_summary: The team discuss porcupines’ self-impaling mishaps and salty habits, smart clothing that can detect body odour (and even control your home), and the perils and oddities of asteroid deflection and tracking. They finish with the wild early days of rugby, when matches could involve hundreds of players and far harsher rules.

facts.fact: Okay, my fact this week is that North American potcups sometimes fall out of trees and impale themselves of their own spice.
My fact is that scientists are developing a t-shirt which tells you when you smell.

And what they discovered in simulation was that it would break apart like it does in the movie. Unfortunately, the gravity is so great that it would just be pulled back together and there's one solid asteroid and just wipe us out.
My fact this week is that until the 1840s, there was no maximum size for a rugby team. So, matches were sometimes played with up to 300 players on the pitch at the same time.
